response.justification: Candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with broad web, mobile, and some embedded-adjacent experience, but the role requires native-level Japanese (not listed), deep C/UNIX embedded firmware work, Linux kernel modification, and LAN-switch security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, switch chips) — none of which are demonstrated in the resume. The hard language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Long senior-level career with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship discipline
Some low-level/systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and D-lang systems work
Comfortable across many languages and stacks, suggesting fast ramp-up ability
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (hard requirement)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, MACsec/802.1X, or Linux kernel modification experience
Career trajectory is web/SaaS/full-stack, not network security or embedded systems

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / switch security experience
Senior engineering rigor and C/systems exposure
